2026-04-16T23:35:12.8974807Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8975364Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8975781Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8976137Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8976443Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8976672Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8976825Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-16T23:35:12.8976999Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-16T23:35:12.8977390Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-16T23:35:12.8978124Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. 2026-04-16T23:36:31.2447057Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T23:36:17.520645302Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58243,\"title\":\"Move all existing NoWarn to inline suppressions\",\"body\":\"# Enforce NoWarn usage in shipping client libraries\\n\\n## Summary\\n\\n`` entries in `.csproj` files are global, unjustified suppressions that bypass analyzer enforcement without SDK team visibility. Today there are **247 files** with NoWarn entries across **71 unique diagnostic codes**. This issue tracks a phased approach to bring these under control.\\n\\nRelated: #57586 (broader suppression validation)\\n\\n## Problem\\n\\n- `` silently disables analyzer rules project-wide with no justification required\\n- The SDK team has no visibility into what\\u0026#39;s being suppressed or why\\n- Critical rules (async correctness, API design guidelines) are being bypassed\\n- There\\u0026#39;s no mechanism to prevent new suppressions from being added\\n\\n## Plan\\n\\n### Phase 1: Block new NoWarn additions ✅\\n\\nAdd an MSBuild target (`ValidateNoWarn`) that runs before `CoreCompile` for all shipping client libraries. The target:\\n\\n- Parses `$(NoWarn)` at build time\\n- Subtracts centrally-defined repo-wide codes (from `eng/Directory.Build.Common.props`)\\n- Subtracts per-project grandfathered codes (allow list in `eng/NoWarnValidation.targets`)\\n- Errors on any remaining unapproved codes\\n\\nAll ~153 existing projects with NoWarn entries are grandfathered in the initial allow list. New additions are blocked immediately.\\n\\n**PR**: (link to PR)\\n\\n### Phase 2: Migrate existing NoWarn to inline suppressions\\n\\nRemove allow list entries one project at a time, each in its own PR. For each project:\\n\\n1. Remove the project\\u0026#39;s entry from `eng/NoWarnValidation.targets`\\n2. For each NoWarn code in that project:\\n   - **Fix the underlying issue** if feasible, or\\n   - **Move to `#pragma warning disable`** with a justification comment, or\\n   - **Move to `[SuppressMessage]`** with a `Justification` parameter\\n3. Verify the project builds\\n\\nThis can be done incrementally by anyone — no coordination needed. Good candidate for first-time contributors or as part of regular package maintenance.\\n\\n\\nTop NoWarn codes by occurrence (migration priority)\\n\\n| Code | Count | Description |\\n|------|-------|-------------|\\n| CS1591 | 126 | Missing XML doc comment |\\n| AZC0035 | 22 | Missing model factory method |\\n| AZC0012 | 20 | Single word type names |\\n| AZC0034 | 19 | Duplicate type names |\\n| SA1636 | 9 | File header copyright text |\\n| OPENAI001 | 9 | OpenAI experimental types |\\n| 3021 | 9 | CLS-compliant methods |\\n| AZC0030 | 6 | Improper model name suffix |\\n| AZC0007 | 6 | Minimal constructor |\\n\\n\\n\\n### Phase 3: Centralize remaining suppressions\\n\\nAfter Phase 2, some codes may remain that genuinely apply project-wide and don\\u0026#39;t make sense as inline suppressions (e.g., `CS1591` across dozens of provisioning libraries). For these:\\n\\n1. Move the remaining per-project NoWarn entries out of individual `.csproj` files into a central policy file (e.g., `eng/NoWarnPolicy.props`)\\n2. The central file becomes the single source of truth — projects are not allowed to set `` directly\\n3. Any changes to the central file are visible in PR review and owned by the SDK team\\n\\nThis phase is optional and depends on what\\u0026#39;s left after Phase 2. If migration eliminates most entries, centralization may not be needed.\\n\\n## Design Details\\n\\n### What\\u0026#39;s enforced\\n\\n- **Scope**: Shipping client libraries only (`IsShippingClientLibrary == \\u0026#39;true\\u0026#39;`)\\n- **Skipped**: Test projects, samples, perf projects, stress tests, non-client libraries\\n- **Skipped**: Design-time builds (no IDE slowdown)\\n\\n### Escape hatches\\n\\n- `NoWarnValidationMode=disabled` — Skip validation entirely (emergency use)\\n- `NoWarnValidationMode=record` — Audit mode: logs effective NoWarn codes without erroring\\n- Add an entry to `eng/NoWarnValidation.targets` — Grandfather a new exception (requires PR review)\\n\\n### Error message\\n\\n```\\nerror AZSDK0002: Unapproved NoWarn codes in : .\\nDo not add NoWarn entries to project files. Instead, use\\n#pragma warning disable with a justification comment, or\\n[SuppressMessage] with a Justification parameter.\\n```\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58243\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-redding\",\"id\":66138537,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-redding\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66138537?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-redding\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-16T23:32:18Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-16T23:32:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-16T23:36:31.2464155Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T23:36:17.532457375Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58243,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-16T23:36:31.2483833Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T23:36:17.707589782Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58243,\"title\":\"Move all existing NoWarn to inline suppressions\",\"body\":\"# Enforce NoWarn usage in shipping client libraries\\n\\n## Summary\\n\\n`` entries in `.csproj` files are global, unjustified suppressions that bypass analyzer enforcement without SDK team visibility. Today there are **247 files** with NoWarn entries across **71 unique diagnostic codes**. This issue tracks a phased approach to bring these under control.\\n\\nRelated: #57586 (broader suppression validation)\\n\\n## Problem\\n\\n- `` silently disables analyzer rules project-wide with no justification required\\n- The SDK team has no visibility into what\\u0026#39;s being suppressed or why\\n- Critical rules (async correctness, API design guidelines) are being bypassed\\n- There\\u0026#39;s no mechanism to prevent new suppressions from being added\\n\\n## Plan\\n\\n### Phase 1: Block new NoWarn additions ✅\\n\\nAdd an MSBuild target (`ValidateNoWarn`) that runs before `CoreCompile` for all shipping client libraries. The target:\\n\\n- Parses `$(NoWarn)` at build time\\n- Subtracts centrally-defined repo-wide codes (from `eng/Directory.Build.Common.props`)\\n- Subtracts per-project grandfathered codes (allow list in `eng/NoWarnValidation.targets`)\\n- Errors on any remaining unapproved codes\\n\\nAll ~153 existing projects with NoWarn entries are grandfathered in the initial allow list. New additions are blocked immediately.\\n\\n**PR**: (link to PR)\\n\\n### Phase 2: Migrate existing NoWarn to inline suppressions\\n\\nRemove allow list entries one project at a time, each in its own PR. For each project:\\n\\n1. Remove the project\\u0026#39;s entry from `eng/NoWarnValidation.targets`\\n2. For each NoWarn code in that project:\\n   - **Fix the underlying issue** if feasible, or\\n   - **Move to `#pragma warning disable`** with a justification comment, or\\n   - **Move to `[SuppressMessage]`** with a `Justification` parameter\\n3. Verify the project builds\\n\\nThis can be done incrementally by anyone — no coordination needed. Good candidate for first-time contributors or as part of regular package maintenance.\\n\\n\\nTop NoWarn codes by occurrence (migration priority)\\n\\n| Code | Count | Description |\\n|------|-------|-------------|\\n| CS1591 | 126 | Missing XML doc comment |\\n| AZC0035 | 22 | Missing model factory method |\\n| AZC0012 | 20 | Single word type names |\\n| AZC0034 | 19 | Duplicate type names |\\n| SA1636 | 9 | File header copyright text |\\n| OPENAI001 | 9 | OpenAI experimental types |\\n| 3021 | 9 | CLS-compliant methods |\\n| AZC0030 | 6 | Improper model name suffix |\\n| AZC0007 | 6 | Minimal constructor |\\n\\n\\n\\n### Phase 3: Centralize remaining suppressions\\n\\nAfter Phase 2, some codes may remain that genuinely apply project-wide and don\\u0026#39;t make sense as inline suppressions (e.g., `CS1591` across dozens of provisioning libraries). For these:\\n\\n1. Move the remaining per-project NoWarn entries out of individual `.csproj` files into a central policy file (e.g., `eng/NoWarnPolicy.props`)\\n2. The central file becomes the single source of truth — projects are not allowed to set `` directly\\n3. Any changes to the central file are visible in PR review and owned by the SDK team\\n\\nThis phase is optional and depends on what\\u0026#39;s left after Phase 2. If migration eliminates most entries, centralization may not be needed.\\n\\n## Design Details\\n\\n### What\\u0026#39;s enforced\\n\\n- **Scope**: Shipping client libraries only (`IsShippingClientLibrary == \\u0026#39;true\\u0026#39;`)\\n- **Skipped**: Test projects, samples, perf projects, stress tests, non-client libraries\\n- **Skipped**: Design-time builds (no IDE slowdown)\\n\\n### Escape hatches\\n\\n- `NoWarnValidationMode=disabled` — Skip validation entirely (emergency use)\\n- `NoWarnValidationMode=record` — Audit mode: logs effective NoWarn codes without erroring\\n- Add an entry to `eng/NoWarnValidation.targets` — Grandfather a new exception (requires PR review)\\n\\n### Error message\\n\\n```\\nerror AZSDK0002: Unapproved NoWarn codes in : .\\nDo not add NoWarn entries to project files. Instead, use\\n#pragma warning disable with a justification comment, or\\n[SuppressMessage] with a Justification parameter.\\n```\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58243\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-redding\",\"id\":66138537,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-redding\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66138537?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-redding\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-16T23:32:18Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-16T23:32:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
